value: 일본 국외 거주 외국인 여권 소지자. 'Temporary Visitor' 체류자격으로 입국한 자만 구매 가능. 일본 국적자 또는 일본 거주 외국인은 구매 불가.

value.비고: 2023년 10월 인상 후 유지. 2025-2026 추가 인상 없음.

value: 한국 국적자는 90일 이내 관광 목적 무비자 입국 가능. 여권 잔여 유효기간 제한 없음 (체류 기간 이상 권장). Visit Japan Web 사전 등록 권장.
Suica/PASMO 등 IC카드로 전철, 버스, 편의점 결제 가능. 물리 카드 재고 부족은 2025.3월 공식 해소. Welcome Suica(관광객용, 28일 유효) 주요 역에서 판매 재개. 모바일 Suica(Apple/Google) 및 Welcome Suica Mobile(iPhone, 180일 유효) 이용 가능.
지정석(Reserved)과 자유석(Non-reserved) 구분. JR Pass 소지 시 지정석 무료 예약 가능 (Nozomi/Mizuho 제외). 자유석은 선착순 탑승.
면세 범위: 주류 3병(760ml 이하), 담배 200개비(거주자)/400개비(비거주자), 향수 2oz, 기타 품목 20만엔 이하. 초과 시 신고 및 관세 부과.

value.비고: 구급차 무료, 치료비는 별도. 119/110은 일본어 우선이나 통역 연결 가능.

value: Visit Japan Web은 웹 기반 서비스(앱 불필요). 여권 정보, 입국심사(하선카드), 세관신고, 면세쇼핑 3개 항목 사전 등록. 등록 후 QR코드 발급 → 공항 도착 시 제시. 2026년 현재 필수는 아니나 강력 권장 (심사 시간 대폭 단축). 스크린샷 불가, 라이브 접속 필요.

신제도_2026_11월.방식: 환급형 (구매 시 세금 포함 → 출국 시 환급)

value: Foreigners visiting Japan can use the Visit Japan Web service for immigration procedures (immigration clearance, customs declaration) and tax-free purchases during their stay in Japan.

Private medical insurance that can be purchased after entering Japan provides sufficient coverage during the stay, including for COVID-19, allowing visitors to seek medical care without paying the bill on-site by sending the bill to an insurance company.
Passengers may receive duty free treatment for 400 cigarettes, 100 cigars, 20 individual packages of heat-not-burn tobacco, or 500 grams of other kinds of tobacco when brought into Japan.
Foreign nationals except nationals of China, Russia, the CIS Countries, Ukraine, Georgia, the Philippines and Viet Nam wishing to come to Japan for a short-term stay require a visa.
The average cost of a ryokan stay is between 15,000 and 30,000 yen per person and night
Himeji Castle plans to charge local residents 1,000 yen and foreign tourists 2,500 yen
Tamatebako restaurant in Shibuya charges different prices for residents of Japan and tourists
Junguria Okinawa theme park plans to charge foreigners and Japanese residents different entry prices starting July 2024

A pasmo passport card can be purchased from ticket offices or ticket vending machines at Narita Airport, Haneda Airport, Tokyo Metro Pass Offices and various metro stations, but no specific price is mentioned.
The Japan Rail Pass is valid on almost all trains operated on the nationwide JR network, including shinkansen, limited express, rapid, and local trains, but generally not valid on non-JR trains.
A full meal in tourist-populated areas in Japan can be found for approximately 10 CAD, including tax, covering fast food and teishoku set meals.

As of 2026, many stations are reducing staffed ticket counters, making IC cards like Suica more time-saving for travelers. Suica can be used nationwide due to Japan’s IC card interoperability system, allowing a Suica purchased in Tokyo to be used on subways in Osaka or buses in Fukuoka.
Many hotels in Japan, especially ryokans, require guests to remove their shoes at the entrance or in their room.
Hotels within walking distance to Kyoto Station

There are ten major IC cards, including Suica and Icoca, that are interchangeably usable in most major cities in Japan. IC cards can also be used for making purchases at many shops and restaurants across Japan. Tap-to-pay is available at stations, allowing travelers to pay fares by tapping their credit card to a reader on the ticket gate.
Tokyo to Kyoto Shinkansen trip takes just over two hours
Kyoto Station area
When eating noodle dishes in Japan, local culture dictates that you slurp rather than chew, which some say enhances the flavor.
Many convenience stores such as 7-Eleven, FamilyMart, and Lawson, as well as some chain restaurants and discount stores, operate 24 hours a day, even on national holidays.
Chureito Pagoda is part of the Arakura Sengen Shrine, located near Mount Fuji
Many convenience stores and some chain restaurants operate 24 hours a day, even on national holidays.
Kita-Shinchi, Osaka

To connect to Wi-Fi in Japan, travelers can use free Wi-Fi services available at major international airports such as Narita, Haneda, Kansai, New Chitose, Centrair, and Fukuoka airports. Connection methods differ by airport, so travelers should check specific airport Wi-Fi information before use.
Filling set meals during lunch hours cost around 1000 yen; lunch boxes (bento) cost around 500 yen; full meals at small fast food restaurants in busy areas can be had for under 1000 yen
In Japanese dining etiquette, saying “itadakimasu” before a meal is a way of expressing gratitude and respect for the culture.
Wi-FiRental.com costs ¥6,600/month with ¥1,100 free discount using code 'cheapo'; Ninja WiFi Infinite Unlimited costs ¥1,980 per day (¥396 with 80% discount for Tokyo Cheapo readers)
Japan Wireless, Saily, and Ubigi are the three dominant providers of Japan eSIMs, with Japan Wireless offering optional insurance and a refund guarantee if the eSIM does not work.

Booking a connectivity solution like NINJA WiFi before arriving in Japan allows travelers to move directly from customs to transportation without delays and ensures dependable internet access throughout the trip.
In 2018, cash payments made up 82% of all payments in Japan
Customers can use mobile payments in Japan by downloading an app that supports mobile payments and displaying a QR code on their mobile device for businesses to scan and request payment.
Many stores and facilities in Japan only accept cash payments, including temples, shrines, and festival stalls, making cash the norm in these places.
You can buy Japan Travel SIM cards like the IIJmio Japan Travel SIM at BIC Camera shops in Tokyo, which have the best selection and offer SIM card installation for ¥2200.
Travelers should review coverage maps during trip planning to ensure reliable mobile internet access in Japan.
Wise Multi-Currency Card charges a small, upfront fee and uses the mid-market exchange rate for currency conversion
Visa and Mastercard credit cards are widely accepted in Japan for purchases, payments, and ATM withdrawals.
Travelers should be prepared to pay with cash as an alternative to credit cards because payment errors may occur at some locations.
The cheapest SIM card plan costs 4,730 yen for 8 days with 25GB data
The Tokaido Shinkansen connects Tokyo and Osaka in about four hours.
The Shinkansen bullet train, introduced in 1964 for the Tokyo Olympics, connects Tokyo and Osaka in about four hours, providing high speed and dependability.
The only card-type IC card available for travelers to purchase is the Welcome Suica, which can be bought at designated locations and charged at black multifunction ticket machines.

Pasmo and Suica are rechargeable prepaid rail IC cards widely used in the Tokyo area and can be purchased at ticket vending machines in stations throughout Japan including major airports such as Tokyo’s Narita and Haneda.
Many hotels in Japan, especially ryokans, require guests to remove their shoes at the entrance or in their room.
Kansai region, Japan

Internet cafés typically cost between ¥1500 and ¥3000 per person per night as a budget accommodation option in Japan.
Traditional types of accommodation in Japan, such as ryokan, typically charge per guest rather than per room.
House and apartment rentals are available all over Japan

A balanced Japanese meal should satisfy the five basic tastes: sweetness, saltiness, sourness, bitterness, and umami.
In Japan, there is an expectation in some restaurants that you will order additional food and drinks over the course of the meal, so the check may not be brought to you until you ask for it.
Saying “itadakimasu” before a meal is a way of expressing gratitude in Japanese dining etiquette, which shows respect for the culture and helps preserve Japanese heritage.
Tokyo
Most convenience stores in Japan are open 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, especially in urban areas.

In Japanese convenience stores (konbini), there is a tax distinction between eating in and takeout: eating in requires paying a 10% tax, while takeout is taxed at 8%. Clerks often do not ask customers if they intend to eat in, causing confusion and incorrect tax application.

Japan has more than 50,000 convenience stores known as konbini, operated mainly by major chains such as Seven Eleven, Family Mart, and Lawson, which offer a wide range of meals, snacks, sweets, and alcoholic beverages.

A macrobiotic meal in Japan typically starts with whole grains, especially brown rice, considered the most energetically balanced food, and includes a variety of moderately balanced foods to achieve energetic balance by combining 'yang' foods like animal protein with 'yin' foods like vegetables.

To use the golf feature on the Amazfit Balance 2 watch, download golf course maps via the Zepp App by selecting courses and tapping Download. On the watch, access Golf mode by pressing the lower button to open the App List and tapping Golf, or by pressing the digital crown to open the Workout List and tapping Golf. After launching Golf mode, select a course and complete initial setup including handedness, units, tee distance, scoring method, and tee box selection before starting.

To use a Japan prepaid travel SIM card, insert the SIM card into your device only after arriving in Japan; the SIM will automatically activate upon first use in Japan. For iOS devices, go to Settings - Mobile - Mobile Data Options and toggle Data Roaming to On. For Android devices, go to Connections - Mobile networks and toggle Data Roaming to On.

Kyoto is a historic city in Japan, located on the main island of Honshu.
Mt Fuji is located near Tokyo and can be visited on a day trip from Tokyo.

Fukuoka is Kyushu’s largest city and an international port connecting Japan to much of Asia.
